'''Groff Haugg''' was an [[Unidentified four-breasted horned species|alien]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual who was the owner of [[Haugg Nerf Industries]] and a [[Death stick/Legends|death stick]] trafficker.

==Biography==
The [[Unidentified four-breasted horned species|alien]] known as Haugg ran a [[Nerf/Legends|nerf]]-packing plant in the [[Industrial District/Legends|industrial sector]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] prior to the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]] called [[Haugg Nerf Industries]] which supplied nerf meat, but his plant was also a front for the trafficking of [[Death stick/Legends|death sticks]], with the help of the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]] [[Connus Trell]]. The smuggled death sticks, secured from the [[Malastare/Legends|Malastare]] [[Dug/Legends|Dug]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] and death stick producer-distributor [[Sebolto]], contained a [[Bando Gora/Legends|Bando Gora]] neurotoxin that enslaved anyone who used it. He shipped them to various corners of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], smuggling them in the sides of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] shipping containers that contained nerf meat. He was killed while being interrogated by [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]] by carbonite flash-freezing before [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] could get any information from him. However, Groff Haugg lied to Montross by diverting him to the [[Gazzari system/Legends|Gazzari system]], which used to have a death stick smuggling operation before it was stopped by the Republic, something [[Rozatta]] would later comment on by saying, "he was probably more afraid of the Bando Gora then he was of Montross".

==Behind the scenes==
Groff Haugg debut in [[LucasArts]]' [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]'',<ref name="Bounty Hunter" /> which was first released on [[November 22]], [[2002]].<ref name="YBY">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> In the ''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'', Groff Haugg is shown to be a [[Gran/Legends|Gran]];<ref name="Official Strategy Guide">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref> however, the game itself—as well as a concept art by [[Cory Allemeier]]—show Haugg to be a different [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Bounty Hunter" /> Game designer [[Jon Knoles]] would later confirmed Haugg to be the same species as [[Groff Zugga]].<ref name="Jon Knole 1" /><ref name="Jon Knole 2" /> In the [[2008]] [[Del Rey]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', Haugg's name was misspelled as "Groff Haug."<ref name="CSWE" />
